description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has been asked to collect and summarise every “ERROR”-containing log in a small application tree.  
The application resides under  

  /home/user/app  

and its log area looks like:

  /home/user/app/logs  
      ├── auth.log  
      ├── db.log  
      ├── payment.log  
      └── old/  
          └── archive.log  

Your objective is to use find + xargs (and only standard GNU user–land tools that are normally pre-installed in a minimal Linux image) to build a reproducible “evidence bundle” inside a brand-new directory called  

  /home/user/analysis  

Perform ALL of the following subtasks:

1. Create /home/user/analysis if it does not yet exist.

2. Generate a plain-text list file named  

   /home/user/analysis/error_filenames.txt  

   • The file must contain one absolute path per line.  
   • List EVERY *.log file that  
     – resides somewhere under /home/user/app/logs (any depth),  
     – is NOT hidden (its basename must **not** begin with “.”), and  
     – contains at least one line that literally includes the substring “ERROR”.  
   • Use find piped into xargs+grep (or find -exec), not a handcrafted list.  
   • Sort the paths in *strict* byte-wise ascending order before writing them.  
   • Terminate each line with ‘\n’ and do **not** add a blank line at the end of the file.  

3. Pack every file listed in error_filenames.txt into a compressed tarball called  

   /home/user/analysis/error_logs.tar.gz  

   • The tarball should store the files *without* absolute pathname information; i.e. the internal names must start at the first path component after “…/logs/”.  
     Example: “/home/user/app/logs/auth.log” becomes “auth.log” inside the archive.  
   • Do not include any directory entries other than those automatically added by tar.  
   • Use a single tar -cz invocation driven by xargs or the @file option—do not copy files to a temporary folder first.  

4. Create a CSV summary file named  

   /home/user/analysis/error_report.csv  

   with *exactly* the following format (verified line-for-line by the autograder):

      filename,error_count
      <absolute-path-to-first-file>,<integer>
      <absolute-path-to-second-file>,<integer>
      …
      TOTAL,<sum-of-all-error_counts>

   • Use the same ordering of filenames that appears in error_filenames.txt.  
   • The header row MUST be exactly “filename,error_count”.  
   • The final TOTAL row MUST be present, using the literal word “TOTAL” (upper-case) in the first field and the grand total of ERROR lines in the second field.  
   • Lines end with ‘\n’; **no** extra blank line after the TOTAL row.  
   • Generate the per-file counts programmatically with grep ‑c, awk, or similar, piped together via xargs. Do not hard-code numbers.

Hints & constraints (will NOT be graded directly but illustrate expected approach):
• Typical one-liner patterns involve find -print0 … | xargs -0 …   
• Remember that dash (-) may be needed for xargs when feeding filenames into tar or grep.  
• The testing harness will inspect:
  – the exact text of error_filenames.txt  
  – that the tarball exists and contains only the correct files at the correct relative paths  
  – the precise CSV layout and numeric totals in error_report.csv  

No sudo or root privileges are available, and you should not try to modify anything outside /home/user.  
You may run intermediate commands, preview output, etc., but only the final artefacts mentioned above are graded.
truth:
Initial file system (before the agent starts):

/home/user/app/logs/auth.log  
[2024-05-01 10:00:00] INFO User login succeeded  
[2024-05-01 10:01:00] ERROR Invalid password for user 'bob'  
[2024-05-01 10:02:00] ERROR User 'alice' account locked  
[2024-05-01 10:03:00] INFO Session ended  
[2024-05-01 10:04:00] INFO User logout  

/home/user/app/logs/db.log  
[2024-05-01 12:00:00] INFO Connection pool initialised  
[2024-05-01 12:01:00] INFO Query executed successfully  
[2024-05-01 12:02:00] INFO Connection closed  

/home/user/app/logs/payment.log  
[2024-05-02 08:00:00] ERROR Payment gateway timeout  
[2024-05-02 08:00:01] ERROR Retry attempt failed  
[2024-05-02 08:00:02] INFO Payment fallback success  
[2024-05-02 08:00:03] INFO Receipt sent  
[2024-05-02 08:00:04] ERROR Payment record missing  
[2024-05-02 08:00:05] INFO Audit complete  

/home/user/app/logs/old/archive.log  
[2024-04-25 00:00:00] INFO Archival started  
[2024-04-25 00:00:01] INFO Reading tables  
[2024-04-25 00:00:02] ERROR Disk full during archival  
[2024-04-25 00:00:03] INFO Archival aborted  

There are **no** pre-existing files or directories named /home/user/analysis*.

Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. /home/user/analysis/error_filenames.txt  
Exact contents (including final newlines, no trailing blank line):
/home/user/app/logs/auth.log
/home/user/app/logs/old/archive.log
/home/user/app/logs/payment.log

2. /home/user/analysis/error_logs.tar.gz  
   • A valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
   • tar -tzf /home/user/analysis/error_logs.tar.gz yields exactly:  
       auth.log  
       old/archive.log  
       payment.log  
     (The order of entries is not graded.)

3. /home/user/analysis/error_report.csv  
Exact contents (including newlines, no trailing blank line):
filename,error_count
/home/user/app/logs/auth.log,2
/home/user/app/logs/old/archive.log,1
/home/user/app/logs/payment.log,3
TOTAL,6

Permissions: every file inside /home/user/analysis must be readable by “user” (0600 or 0644 are both acceptable).

The autograder will FAIL if:
• Any hidden *.log files are included.  
• db.log appears in any artefact.  
• Ordering or numeric totals deviate from the specification above.  
• The tarball paths are absolute or include extra directory prefixes.
